title: Spreading Smiles on Random Acts of Kindness Day
story: People all over the world make February 17 extra special by doing surprise good deeds for others! On Random Acts of Kindness Day, kids might leave encouraging notes in library books, help carry groceries, or bake cookies for neighbors. Adults pay for strangers' coffee, shovel snowy sidewalks, or donate clothes to shelters. Schools organize kindness challenges where students compete to do the most helpful acts. Social media fills with photos of people's good deeds, inspiring others to join in. The best part? These random acts often create chain reactions, where one kind gesture leads to another and another, making the whole day brighter for everyone involved.
activity_question: What might kids do to celebrate Random Acts of Kindness Day?
activity_choices: Leave encouraging notes in library books
activity_answer: Leave encouraging notes in library books
follow_up_question: What random act of kindness would make your day if someone did it for you?

title: Cabbage Gets Its Day to Shine!
story: Every February 17, people celebrate National Cabbage Day by giving this crunchy green vegetable some serious love! Restaurants serve up cabbage rolls, coleslaw, and sauerkraut specials. Home cooks try new cabbage recipes like stir-fries and soups. Some people share cabbage facts on social media or post pictures of their favorite cabbage dishes. It's the perfect day to discover how tasty this leafy veggie can be!
activity_question: What are some ways people celebrate National Cabbage Day?
activity_choices: Making cabbage recipes and sharing photos online
Planting cabbage seeds in their gardens
activity_answer: Making cabbage recipes and sharing photos online
bonus_fact: Cabbage comes in purple, green, and even white varieties!

title: Creamy Coffee Dreams Day
story: Coffee lovers everywhere celebrate National Cafe Au Lait Day on February 17! This special drink mixes equal parts coffee and steamed milk, creating a creamy treat that's smoother than regular coffee. People visit cafes, make their own at home, or try new flavors to mark this delicious day.
activity_question: What does cafe au lait mix together?
follow_up_question: What other coffee drinks would you like to try?

title: My Way Day: Do It Your Way!
story: February 17 is My Way Day, when people celebrate doing things exactly how they want to! This fun holiday encourages everyone to break free from boring routines and try something different. People might eat dessert for breakfast, wear mismatched socks, or take a new route to school. Some families let kids pick the dinner menu or choose a crazy movie to watch together. It's all about being yourself and making your own choices for one whole day!
activity_question: What might someone do on My Way Day?
activity_choices: Do only what others tell them to do
follow_up_question: If you could do anything your way today, what would you choose?

title: Sweet Spiced Pudding Day!
story: Every February 17, Americans celebrate National Indian Pudding Day by making this sweet colonial dessert! Indian pudding gets its name from cornmeal, which colonists called "Indian meal." People bake it with molasses, milk, and warm spices like cinnamon. Many New England restaurants serve it hot with vanilla ice cream on top!
activity_question: What ingredient gives Indian pudding its name?
bonus_fact: Indian pudding takes about 3 hours to bake slowly in the oven!

title: Going Nuts for National Almond Day!
story: Crunch time arrives every February 17 when almond lovers everywhere celebrate National Almond Day! People munch on raw almonds, bake almond cookies, and whip up creamy almond butter treats. Health fans love sharing how almonds pack protein and good fats, while bakers go wild making almond cakes and marzipan sweets. Social media fills with almond recipe videos and fun facts about these tasty tree nuts that grow in sunny California orchards.
activity_question: What do people commonly make with almonds on National Almond Day?
activity_choices: Almond ice cream and almond pasta
bonus_fact: It takes about 1,000 almonds to make just one jar of almond butter!
